steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The y-intercept is where the curve crosses the y-axis, which occurs at $x = 0$. Since $x$ represents the number of months after purchase, setting $x = 0$ means the moment of purchase. The y-intercept therefore shows the tablet's initial value.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Looking at the graph at $x = 0$, the curve begins at $y = 225$ dollars.

steps.title: Interpret in context
steps.content: The y-intercept value of 225 represents what the tablet was worth at $x = 0$ months after purchase—that is, at the moment it was purchased. That matches choice A — A is correct.
